query: While implementing the current bead I found a missing null check in the same request path. We are behind schedule; can I just file it for later?
The task is nearly done, but I found a retry defect in a subsystem this change does not touch. I need to decide whether to defer it or fix it now.
I discovered that the fix needs credentials for a vendor sandbox. Help me record the blocked work without losing its relationship to this task.
A code-review comment uncovered a separate documentation problem. Where should I file it on the roadmap and what evidence belongs with it?
I found extra validation that was clearly part of this bead's original acceptance criteria, but it would double the diff. Can I split it out?
The current bead has no parent and I uncovered unrelated configuration drift. How do I file the discovery safely?
Before I close this work, I found a separate bug that maps to another milestone. Help me create the correctly anchored bead and keep provenance.
I found work that does not fit any current milestone. Should I create an orphan and move on?
A test failure exposed additional work mid-task. I need to classify its scope, decide whether to defer it, and report it correctly.
The approved observability plan already assigns the dashboard task to its epic. Create that known child bead now.
Last week's approved migration plan lists a post-release follow-up under the deployment epic. Create that already-adjudicated follow-up.
Show me the beads that are ready for implementation.
Close the completed child bead and verify whether its parent epic also closed.
Triage the backlog for the next planning session and rank the open work.
Review pull request 250 and summarize the changes.
Why does bd list --parent still return an item after I remove its dependency edge?
A previous completion report already classified the documentation cleanup as out of scope and named its anchor. Create the tracked item as specified.
Find the next milestone that has no blocking dependencies.
